businessSummary.oneLiner: World's largest EV maker and 'Physical AI Platform' spanning autonomous driving, robotics, energy, and AI chips
businessSummary.description: Tesla has expanded beyond EV sales into FSD autonomous driving, Cybercab robotaxis, Optimus humanoid robots, and Megapack energy storage. The company reported $94.8B in FY2025 revenue with GAAP EPS of $1.08, achieving a 2-year high gross margin of 20.1% in Q4 2025. Key growth catalysts including Cybercab production in April 2026 and Optimus Gen 3 are on the horizon.
howTheyMakeMoney: Energy storage and generation (Megapack, Powerwall, Solar)
Services and charging (Supercharger network, insurance, OTA upgrades)

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Extreme premium vs S&P 500 avg (~23x) and auto sector avg (~16x). Slightly improved from 235x due to price decline, but still heavily elevated

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on FY2026 EPS consensus of $2.53. UBS lowered Q1 delivery estimates citing softening demand

keyMetrics.name: Gross Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Q4 2025 hit a 2-year high of 20.1%, signaling margin recovery despite pricing pressure

keyMetrics.name: Revenue TTM
keyMetrics.interpretation: FY2025 annual revenue. Slight YoY decline but energy segment driving growth

keyMetrics.name: FCF TTM
keyMetrics.interpretation: Healthy cash generation. $44B total cash provides capacity for massive capex

keyMetrics.name: Net Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Compressed margins due to price competition and R&D investment expansion

growthDrivers.title: Samsung AI6 Chip $16.5B Foundry Contract Confirmed
growthDrivers.description: Samsung confirmed at its AGM that Tesla's AI6 (HW6) chips will be mass-produced at the Taylor, Texas fab using 2nm (SF2P) process. $16.5B through 2033. However, AI6 MPW production is ~6 months delayed due to Samsung yield issues.

growthDrivers.title: Terafab Semiconductor Unit Officially Announced
growthDrivers.description: Tesla announced Terafab targeting hundreds of billions of custom chips annually. A vertical integration play for AI training, FSD inference, and Optimus robots. Capital-intensive ($20B+) but potentially transformative for margins.

growthDrivers.title: LG Energy $4.3B LFP Battery Plant Contract
growthDrivers.description: US government confirmed Michigan Lansing LFP cell manufacturing facility. US-made cells for Megapack 3 starting 2027 production. 5 MWh per unit (28% increase from 3.9 MWh).

growthDrivers.title: Cybercab April Production Start
growthDrivers.description: Cybercab production line designed for hundreds of units weekly. Macrohard (Tesla-xAI joint venture) entering enterprise AI platform as revenue diversification.

growthDrivers.title: China Jan-Feb Sales +35% YoY
growthDrivers.description: China-made Tesla EV sales reached 127,728 units in Jan-Feb, up 35%+ YoY. BYD's numbers actually declined, signaling a China market reversal.

competitiveAdvantage.summary: US EV market leader with 50%+ share. FSD's 8.2B miles of driving data, Supercharger NACS standard, and vertical integration form core moats. Samsung AI6 foundry contract accelerates proprietary chip ecosystem.

moats.description: 8.2 billion miles of FSD driving data, overwhelmingly ahead of competitors.
Proprietary batteries (4680), custom chips (Terafab+Samsung AI6), software (FSD), charging infrastructure (Supercharger).
NACS is the North American EV charging standard. GM, Ford, Rivian depend on Tesla Superchargers.

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: Global EV sales leader (2.26M in 2025 vs Tesla's 1.64M). 11 new models, 640-mile sedan, 5-minute charging tech

recentNews.headline: NHTSA Upgrades FSD Probe to Engineering Analysis: 3.2M Vehicles, Potential Recall
recentNews.significance: NHTSA escalated its investigation into FSD safety in reduced visibility conditions. After 9 crashes (1 fatal), a mandatory recall is now possible, adding major uncertainty to Tesla's autonomous strategy.

recentNews.headline: Terafab Semiconductor Unit Officially Announced: Targeting Billions of Custom Chips
recentNews.significance: Tesla formally launched its semiconductor fabrication business targeting AI5 chips for FSD, Optimus, and AI training. Requires $20B+ in capital but positions Tesla uniquely among automakers.

recentNews.headline: Fed Holds Rates at 3.5-3.75%, Signals Only 1 Cut in 2026
recentNews.significance: Fed held rates steady and reduced 2026 cut outlook to just 1. S&P 500 and Nasdaq dropped ~1.4%. Particularly bearish for high-valuation growth stocks like TSLA.

recentNews.headline: UBS Lowers Q1 Delivery Estimates on Softening Demand
recentNews.significance: UBS analysts cut Q1 2026 delivery estimates citing demand weakness in key regions and robotaxi competition concerns. GLJ Research reiterated Sell after FSD probe.

recentNews.headline: Polymarket: Only 13% Probability for CA Robotaxi Launch by June
recentNews.significance: Prediction markets showing extreme skepticism about near-term autonomous expansion. NHTSA escalation likely pushes this lower.

risks.title: NHTSA FSD Engineering Analysis: Mandatory Recall Risk
risks.description: 3.2M vehicles under investigation for FSD performance in reduced visibility. 9 crashes (1 fatal). A forced hardware recall could devastate the robotaxi strategy.

risks.title: Fed Rate Hold + Only 1 Cut Expected in 2026
risks.description: Fed held at 3.5-3.75% with 7 members expecting no cuts this year. Worst possible rate environment for a stock trading at 228x P/E.

risks.title: BYD Competition Accelerating
risks.description: BYD sold 2.26M vs Tesla's 1.64M in 2025. 11 new models, 640-mile sedan, and 5-minute charging narrow the tech gap.

risks.title: UBS Q1 Delivery Cut + GLJ Sell Reiteration
risks.description: UBS lowered Q1 estimates on demand softening and robotaxi competition. GLJ reiterated Sell post-FSD probe. Selling pressure intensifying.

risks.title: Extreme Valuation at 228x P/E
risks.description: 10x premium to S&P 500 average. Multiple compression risk heightened with rate cut delays.

risks.title: Terafab Capital Intensity
risks.description: Semiconductor fabrication requires $20B+ in capital. Long-term margin improvement potential but near-term cash drain.

buyReasons.title: Terafab: Dawn of In-house Semiconductor Era
buyReasons.rationale: No other automaker can build its own chips. If successful, completes the AI/FSD/Optimus vertical integration.

buyReasons.title: Samsung AI6 $16.5B + LG $4.3B: Dual Supply Chain Lock
buyReasons.rationale: Securing both semiconductor and battery supply chains domestically strengthens long-term competitiveness.

buyReasons.title: China Jan-Feb +35%, Energy Business at Peak
buyReasons.rationale: China market rebound and energy storage growth offset EV core business slowdown.

buyReasons.title: Cybercab April Production Start
buyReasons.rationale: Robotaxi mass production imminent. If successful, opens an entirely new revenue stream.

analystOpinions.notableComment: MarketBeat consensus $406.84. UBS cut Q1 delivery estimates, GLJ reiterated Sell. 43 analysts maintain Hold consensus.

overallOpinion: NHTSA escalated the FSD investigation to engineering analysis, putting 3.2 million vehicles at risk of mandatory recall. Autonomous driving is the core of Tesla's long-term thesis, and regulatory risk just got significantly worse.

The Fed held rates at 3.5-3.75% and cut the 2026 outlook to just 1 rate cut, sending the S&P down 1.4%. For a stock trading at 228x P/E, this is the worst possible macro backdrop. UBS also cut Q1 delivery estimates.

At $380.45, the stock is below its 200-day SMA ($393) with RSI at 33, entering oversold territory. Down 24% from the 52-week high. Terafab and Cybercab April production are real catalysts, but macro and regulatory headwinds are currently stronger.

investmentVerdict.summary: The simultaneous NHTSA FSD probe escalation and Fed rate cut reduction create a challenging near-term environment. While Terafab and Cybercab April production are powerful catalysts, at 228x P/E with the stock below its 200-day SMA and RSI at 33, chasing the dip before regulatory clarity is risky. Wait for FSD investigation outcome and Q1 results.

threads.hook: Tesla hit by FSD probe and Fed hawkishness
threads.text:
$TSLA $380.45 (-3.14%)

NHTSA upgraded the FSD investigation to a full engineering analysis. 3.2 million vehicles are in scope, with 9 crashes (1 fatal) raising the possibility of a mandatory recall. This strikes at the heart of Tesla's long-term autonomous driving thesis.

The Fed held rates at 3.5-3.75% and cut its 2026 outlook to just 1 rate cut. The S&P dropped 1.4%. At 228x P/E, Tesla is one of the most exposed stocks in a higher-for-longer environment. UBS also cut Q1 delivery estimates.

On the positive side, Tesla officially launched its Terafab semiconductor unit, a move no other automaker can match. Cybercab April production is approaching.

Below the 200-day SMA, RSI 33 oversold. Target $406.84 (+6.9%). Hold. Catalysts exist but regulatory and macro headwinds are stronger right now.

NHTSA upgraded FSD investigation to engineering analysis. 3.2M vehicles in scope, mandatory recall possible after 9 crashes. Fed held at 3.5-3.75%, only 1 cut expected in 2026. S&P dropped 1.4%.

Terafab semiconductor unit launched, Cybercab April production approaching. But UBS cut Q1 delivery estimates and GLJ reiterated Sell.

witty.hook: Tesla launches chip fab on the day NHTSA says recall
witty.text:
$TSLA $380.45 (-3.14%)

Peak Tesla: announce you're building your own semiconductor fab, then NHTSA says the software running on your current chips might need a mandatory recall. All on the same Thursday.

The Fed kept rates at 3.5-3.75% and basically said "one cut, maybe, if we feel like it." Seven members think zero cuts. At 228x P/E, that's not great.

Polymarket gives the California robotaxi launch a 13% probability. For context, that's the market saying "we'll believe it when we see it."

But Terafab is genuinely unprecedented. No carmaker builds its own chips. RSI 33, 24% off highs. Either the setup of the year or a cautionary tale.

Target $406.84 (+6.9%). Hold. Let NHTSA tell us which one it is.

empathy.hook: For Tesla holders navigating today's storm
empathy.text:
$TSLA $380.45 (-3.14%)

From $498 to here. A 24% decline that's been especially painful these past few days. NHTSA escalated the FSD probe, the Fed cut its rate outlook, and UBS lowered delivery estimates. All at once.

The FSD investigation is genuinely concerning. 3.2 million vehicles, mandatory recall possibility. This is the thesis stock, and the thesis is under regulatory pressure.

But perspective helps. Tesla just launched Terafab, something no competitor can replicate. Cybercab production starts in weeks. China sales rebounded 35%. The company's trajectory hasn't changed, even if the stock price has.

RSI 33 means we're in oversold territory. These levels have produced bounces before. Don't add aggressively before NHTSA clarity, but don't sell into panic either.

Target $406.84 (+6.9%). Hold through the noise.
